text.title: Employer Branding Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every employer-branding artefact (audit, content calendar, testimonial, review response) must satisfy before external publication.

rule.statement: Every external-facing brand asset (LinkedIn post, careers page block, testimonial, ad) MUST name exactly one EVP pillar (Compensation, Benefits, Career, Work Environment, or Culture/Purpose) and link to the proof points behind it. Assets that do not name a pillar are rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Untraced assets become "vibe" copy that contradicts hiring conversations and offer letters; pillar-anchoring forces consistency between brand and EVP and gives recruiters a citation when candidates question a claim.

rule.statement: Employee testimonials MUST be attributed to a named human (first name + role + tenure band) and carry a written consent record. Anonymous or fabricated quotes are rejected; "an engineer said …" without a record is treated as fabrication.
rule.rationale: Anonymous testimonials are presumed manufactured by readers and frequently violate consent policies; named attribution is both the integrity check and the legal record.

rule.statement: Diversity, compensation-band, and retention statistics MUST be supplied by HRIS-sourced data; if unavailable at draft time, insert the literal token "UNVERIFIED" so a human fills it from HRIS before publication. Agents must NEVER synthesize these numbers.
rule.rationale: Synthesized DEI / pay numbers create reputation incidents the moment an employee contradicts them; the UNVERIFIED placeholder is fail-loud and forces the human checkpoint.

rule.statement: Glassdoor, Indeed, and Comparably reviews MUST receive a drafted response within 48 hours of posting. The draft is reviewed by the named brand owner before posting; responses that breach the SLA are flagged in the weekly brand metrics report.
rule.rationale: Unanswered reviews compound; a brand that responds within 48h is read as engaged regardless of star rating, while silence is read as confirmation. Tracking the SLA in metrics keeps drift visible.

rule.statement: A post-pass MUST strip generic clichés ("innovative", "passionate", "fast-paced", "world-class", "rockstar", "ninja") from every draft asset. Assets shipping with banned tokens are returned for rewrite.
rule.rationale: LLM-default phrases add word count without signal and are the most common reason candidates label brand copy "corporate speak"; the strip-pass is mechanical and removes the lowest-quality copy without human time.

rule.statement: If the Applies-If preconditions fail OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or to no methodology at all).
rule.rationale: Mis-application of a methodology produces worse artefacts than no methodology at all because downstream consumers trust the schema and act on bad data. The skip-record makes the non-application traceable so future audits can detect drift.
